ground_truth.description: Photo of a statue of a sailor kissing a nurse from Omaha Memorial. Part of art installation "Unconditional Surrender". Military ship (possibly aircraft carrier) visible in background.
ground_truth.photographer_description: A photo of a statue of a sailor kissing a nurse from Omaha Memorial. It's part of an art installation called «Unconditional Surrender». A military ship (possibly aircraft carrier, USS Midway Museum) is visible in the background. The location is San Diego Harbor / Tuna Harbor Park. This could be tricky for two reasons: 1) the statue is quite realistic, so it might think it's two real people kissing and not a statue, and 2) it's inspired by an iconic V-J Day photo and might confuse with the original. The statue exists in multiple locations.

details.description: A monumental figurative sculpture of a U.S. Navy sailor dipping and kissing a nurse stands on a circular plinth along a waterfront promenade. The figures are polychrome and highly realistic: the sailor in dark uniform and white cap; the nurse in a white dress and heels. Inscribed pavers and low interpretive signs surround the base. Behind the statue is San Diego Bay with a large gray aircraft carrier moored along the pier, and low hills across the water. Clear daylight with blue sky emphasizes the scale; the photo is taken from a low angle close to the sculpture.

entities.name: Unconditional Surrender (a.k.a. Embracing Peace)
entities.notes: 25-ft monumental sculpture by Seward Johnson, installed 2013 at Tuna Harbor Park, San Diego; painted bronze (replacing earlier fiberglass version). Inspired by Alfred Eisenstaedt’s 1945 V-J Day in Times Square photograph (also similar to Victor Jorgensen’s photo).

details.description: A waterfront public sculpture in San Diego depicting two lovers kissing: a sailor in a dark uniform embracing a nurse in a white dress and cap, standing on a circular pedestal on a paved promenade along San Diego Bay. In the background is a large aircraft carrier hull moored at the bay, with a clear blue sky overhead. The scene resembles the Embarcadero area near Seaport Village and the USS Midway Museum, with water, boats, and a promenade suitable for strolling. The sculpture recreates the iconic World War II-era kiss from the famous photograph.

details.description: A low-angle, full shot captures the monumental sculpture 'Unconditional Surrender' (also known as 'Embracing Peace') situated on a paved plaza. The 25-foot statue depicts a sailor in a dark navy dress blue uniform dipping and kissing a nurse in a white dress, white stockings, and white shoes, recreating the iconic 1945 V-J Day photograph. The figures stand on a circular concrete base. To the right, the massive grey hull and flight deck of the USS Midway aircraft carrier are visible, with small figures of people lining the deck railing. The background features the calm, deep blue water of San Diego Bay and a distant shoreline under a bright blue sky with wispy clouds. The ground is paved with a two-tone grey checkered brick pattern.

details.description: A large-scale sculpture titled 'Unconditional Surrender' dominates the foreground, depicting a sailor in a dark navy uniform kissing a woman in a white nurse's dress and shoes. The statue is a 3D recreation of the famous V-J Day in Times Square photograph. It stands on a circular concrete base within a paved public area featuring a checkered stone pattern. In the background, the massive grey hull of the USS Midway aircraft carrier is docked in the water. The setting is a waterfront park under a bright blue sky with light, wispy clouds. San Diego Bay and the distant shoreline are visible behind the sculpture.

details.description: A large outdoor figurative sculpture of a World War II–era U.S. Navy sailor dipping and kissing a woman in a white nurse’s uniform, set on a round pedestal with engraved pavers. The scene is on a waterfront promenade under a clear blue sky. At right, the gray hull and flight deck of the aircraft carrier USS Midway (now a museum) are visible across San Diego Bay, confirming a location along the Embarcadero at Tuna Harbor Park.

details.description: A monumental polychrome sculpture of a World War II U.S. Navy sailor dipping and kissing a nurse stands on a circular plinth along a waterfront promenade. The figures are over-life-size, painted to resemble realistic clothing (sailor’s dark uniform and white hat; nurse’s white dress and heels). Plaques and engraved pavers surround the base. Behind the statue, across the water, is a large aircraft carrier museum ship with its flight deck visible. The setting is an open bayfront park with clear blue sky and distant shoreline.

details.description: A large outdoor sculpture of a sailor in a dark naval uniform embracing and kissing a nurse in a white dress and nurse's cap. They stand on a circular pedestal along a waterfront promenade. Behind them is a massive aircraft carrier docked at a pier, with clear blue sky above. The scene references a iconic WWII-era moment, with the figures leaning and the nurse standing on tiptoe as if mid-kiss, set against the maritime backdrop of a harbor.

details.description: A full-shot, eye-level photograph of the monumental sculpture 'Unconditional Surrender' (also known as 'Embracing Peace') situated on a waterfront promenade. The sculpture depicts a colossal sailor in a dark navy dress uniform dipping and kissing a nurse in a white uniform, mimicking the famous V-J Day photograph. The figures stand on a circular concrete pedestal surrounded by a checkered grey and white paved plaza. To the right, the massive grey hull and flight deck of an aircraft carrier, the USS Midway, are visible docked in the harbor. The background features the calm blue waters of San Diego Bay, a distant shoreline with city structures, and a bright blue sky with wispy clouds. A small informational plaque on a metal stand is visible to the left of the statue.

details.description: Large outdoor figurative sculpture of a World War II U.S. Navy sailor dipping and kissing a nurse, rendered at monumental scale and painted in realistic colors. The work stands on a circular plinth along a waterfront promenade on a sunny day. Behind it is a moored gray aircraft carrier with an expansive flight deck, identifying the setting as the San Diego Bay waterfront by the USS Midway Museum. The plaza has engraved pavers and interpretive plaques near the base.
